mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: thin vermilion border, wide anterior fontanels, micrognathia, hypertelorism, low-set ears, anteverted nares, short neck, thickened nuchal skin fold, upslantated palpebral fissures, wrist and knee flexion contractures, agenesis of corpus callosum, ventriculomegaly, absent or hypoplastic cerebellar vermis, camptodactyly

intrauterine growth retardation, disproportionate long limbs, pectus excavatum or carinatum, scoliosis, joint hypermobility, lens subluxation, mitral valve prolapse, dilation of the ascending aorta, ectopia lentis

conotruncal cardiac defects, parathyroid gland dysfunction, characteristic facial features including broad forehead, flat midface, narrow jaw, small chin, deep set eyes with epicanthic folds, strabismus, cleft lip and palate, low-set ears, short stature, abnormalities of hands and feet

hepatosplenomegaly, progressive neurological deterioration, cherry red spot, vertical supranuclear gaze palsy, seizures, pulmonary involvement leading to respiratory failure, splenomegaly, jaundice, liver cirrhosis, neurodegenerative symptoms such as cognitive decline, behavior changes, myopathy, peripheral neuropathies

intrauterine growth deficiency, central nervous system damage resulting in learning difficulties, memory problems, attention deficit, impulsivity, hyperactivity, poor coordination, craniofacial anomalies including thin upper lip, flattened midface, smooth philtrum, short palpebral fissures, epicanthic folds, low-set ears, and other structural brain damages

prenatal growth retardation, typical facies characterized by prominent forehead, depressed nasal bridge, hypertelorism, low-set ears, short neck, micropenis, cryptorchidism, radial ray defects, distal arthrogryposis, clubfoot, polydactyly, genitourinary tract anomalies, metaphyseal dysplasia, mental retardation, hearing loss

widening of the anterior fontanelle, frontal bossing, brachymorphic face, short trunk, short neck, kyphoscoliosis, hip dislocation, elbow contractures, waddling gait, delayed dentition, intellectual disability, seizures, increased risk of fractures due to bone fragility

multiple congenital anomalies involving almost all body systems, including severe growth restriction before birth, distinctive facial appearance with downward sloping eyelids, coloboma of irises or optic nerves, microcephaly, prominent ear lobes, cleft lip and palate, polydactyly, clinodactyl, rocker bottom feet, cardiovascular defects, kidney malformations, diaphragmatic hernia, intestinal malrotation, and neural tube defects

distinct facial features consisting of broad thumbs, broad big toes, broad and flat Bridge of the nose, underdeveloped inner eyebrows that may meet in the middle, intellectual disability, short stature, mild to moderate growth hormone deficiency, feeding difficulty during infancy, frequent respiratory infections, sleep apnea, and cardiac defects

obesity starting from early childhood, short stature, hypogonadism, weak muscle tone, especially around the buttocks and hips, insatiable appetite, slow physical and sexual maturation, mild to moderate intellectual disability, small penis size, almond shaped eyes, narrow bifrontal diameter, and a characteristic facial appearance with narrow mouth, downturned corners of the lips, and a narrow bridge of the nose
